mechanism: Blocks several receptor tyrosine kinases including FLT3 and c‑KIT, inhibiting survival and proliferation pathways
additional_notes: Clinically used in renal cell carcinoma (RCC) and gastrointestinal stromal tumors (GIST); reported anti‑proliferative activity in AML lines including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases as well as KIT and other kinases involved in AML cell survival
additional_notes: Used in CML and Philadelphia‑positive ALL; start at lower top dose to limit off‑target toxicity

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL and KIT signaling, reducing proliferation and survival of leukemic cells
additional_notes: Used in CML; some evidence of activity against FLT3 and KIT‑driven AML

mechanism: Potently inhibits BCR‑ABL and FLT3 (including resistant mutants) along with other kinases
additional_notes: Used for resistant CML/ALL; strong FLT3 inhibition with high expected potency in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and other tyrosine kinases, blocking growth signaling in FLT3‑ITD AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as CEP‑701; reported low‑nanomolar FLT3 IC50 in FLT3‑ITD AML including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, suppressing JAK/STAT signaling implicated in AML survival
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera; of interest in AML and in combination with FLT3 inhibitors

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic BCL‑2 family proteins (BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL), enhancing apoptotic cell death
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; in trials for solid and hematologic malignancies; broader BCL‑XL targeting can increase in vitro AML effects

mechanism: Modulates cereblon‑dependent ubiquitination, cytokine production, and angiogenesis, indirectly affecting AML cell growth
additional_notes: Used in multiple myeloma and other indications; direct AML effects modest and usually at mid‑ to high‑micromolar concentrations

mechanism: Cereblon‑binding IMiD that alters ubiquitination of specific substrates, affecting proliferation and differentiation
additional_notes: Used in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma; more potent analog of thalidomide/lenalidomide with activity in some AML models

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I, activating AMPK and inducing energetic stress that can reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Oral antidiabetic drug; IC50 in AML often in low‑millimolar range; prepare fresh in aqueous medium

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing reactive oxygen species and triggering apoptosis
additional_notes: Used for rheumatoid arthritis; induces ROS‑mediated apoptosis in AML including FLT3‑ITD models

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, targeting ALDH‑positive leukemic stem/progenitor cells; activity often enhanced by copper ions
additional_notes: Used for alcohol dependence; commonly combined with CuCl2 (0.5–2 µM constant) for enhanced anti‑leukemic activity; protect from light and prepare fresh

mechanism: Inhibits class I/IIa HDACs, leading to histone hyperacetylation, differentiation, and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as sodium valproate; IC50 in AML often in high‑hundreds‑micromolar to low‑millimolar range; used clinically as an antiepileptic and mood stabilizer

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagic flux, increasing cellular stress and promoting death of AML cells
additional_notes: Often used interchangeably or alongside hydroxychloroquine; IC50 in AML often in the tens of micromolar; also an autophagy inhibitor repurposed in cancer trials
